_comment: TheOrc Foundry recipe TEMPLATE — theorc-router v0. Follow-on #3, explicitly 'or non-LLM' (docs/THEORC_FOUNDRY.md).
phase: pre-F-1 (deterministic routing baseline must exist first)

job.name: theorc-router-smollm2-135m-lora-v0
job.description: Select lane, model, node, or deterministic path for an incoming goal. The docs flag this track as possibly not an LLM at all — a classical classifier over goal features may win outright.

alternates: non-LLM: gradient-boosted classifier over goal features

dataset._dataset_note: Task universe not frozen. Candidate shape: goal + hardware/fleet state → lane/model/node choice. Capture point: RuntimeOrchestrator/OrcScheduler admission decisions once deterministic routing is instrumented.

gates.blocked_reason: Per docs/THEORC_FOUNDRY.md this track starts only after deterministic routing establishes a measured baseline. No deterministic router exists to beat yet — build and measure that first.

stop_rule: If a rule table or classical classifier meets routing accuracy/latency targets, the LLM track stops — this is the track most likely to end in 'the rules win', and that is a success.
